editionData.title: June 24, 2026: Ukraine’s drones are spoiling Putin’s vision for Crimea; Trump dismisses Iran’s rejection of nuclear inspections
editionData.description: Global headlines: Ukraine’s drones are spoiling Putin’s vision for Crimea (Russian-installed authorities in Crimea have halted fuel distribution in vacation season as Ukraine isolates the peninsula); Trump dismisses Iran’s rejection of nuclear inspections (Iran’s Foreign Ministry said there was no plan for the IAEA to inspect its damaged nuclear facilities, a day after Vice President JD Vance said conversations with inspectors could happen imminently); and Keir Starmer is out. Other establishment politicians are in trouble. (Across the West’s advanced democracies, conventional political parties are being gutted by stagnant wages and fraying social contracts that have left voters fed up with the status quo). Markets leaned lower, with 7 of 7 tracked indexes down; NASDAQ Composite down 2.21% was the largest move. Benchmarks tracked: S&P 500, NASDAQ Composite, Euronext 100 Index, and Public Joint-Stock Company Moscow Exchange MICEX-RTS plus 3 more indexes. Hacker News: How the war on terror primed America for autocracy (economist.com: How the war on terror primed America for autocracy); Vulnerability reports are not special anymore (We needed the insight and confidentiality to protect our users, but now that anyone can get the same results from LLM); and Jerry's Map (<span style=\). Product Hunt features OpenArt Director (Direct cinematic videos through chat), Bluerails Discovery (The rails AI agents use to find and pay you), and Blazly SEO (Dominate SEO with an AI content operating system).
editionData.author: Sony Mathew

headlines.title: Ukraine’s drones are spoiling Putin’s vision for Crimea
headlines.summary: Russian-installed authorities in Crimea have halted fuel distribution in vacation season as Ukraine isolates the peninsula.

headlines.title: Trump dismisses Iran’s rejection of nuclear inspections
headlines.summary: Iran’s Foreign Ministry said there was no plan for the IAEA to inspect its damaged nuclear facilities, a day after Vice President JD Vance said conversations with inspectors could happen imminently.

headlines.title: Keir Starmer is out. Other establishment politicians are in trouble.
headlines.summary: Across the West’s advanced democracies, conventional political parties are being gutted by stagnant wages and fraying social contracts that have left voters fed up with the status quo.

headlines.title: U.S. shifts its Latin America approach. Here’s how it’s playing out in Guatemala.
headlines.summary: U.S. lobbyists and pundits with President Donald Trump’s ear are aiding Latin America’s swing to the right.

headlines.title: Who is Andy Burnham, U.K.’s ‘King of the North’ and likely next prime minister?
headlines.summary: The runaway favorite to succeed Prime Minister Keir Starmer is a longtime mayor equally comfortable in the corridors of Westminster and in working-class northern England.

headlines.title: US, Iran at odds over nuclear oversight, Strait of Hormuz after Swiss talks
headlines.summary: US and Iran offer conflicting statements as negotiators push towards reaching a final, comprehensive deal.

headlines.title: Protesters sentenced to decades in US prison over alleged antifa ties
headlines.summary: Civil liberties advocates have expressed alarm over the case involving a protest outside Prairieland Detention Center.

headlines.title: US Supreme Court says Rastafarian man shaved by prison guards can’t sue
headlines.summary: The high court has upheld ruling that prisoners cannot sue prison staff for money damages under religious liberties law.

headlines.title: UN starts evacuating 11,000 stranded sailors from Strait of Hormuz
headlines.summary: Following the start of the US-Israel war on Iran on February 28, Tehran had effectively closed off the vital waterway.

headlines.title: Alibaba sues US military over labelling it a ‘Chinese military company’
headlines.summary: E-commerce giant says that claims of affiliation with Chinese military have 'no basis in fact or law'.

headlines.title: Mamdani's political gamble pays off as his endorsed candidates sweep their primaries
headlines.summary: All three progressive candidates backed by New York Mayor Zohran Mamdani — including two who took on Democratic incumbents — won their primaries in safe seats almost guaranteeing their election in November.

headlines.title: Supreme Court rules that prison guards can't be sued for shaving Rastafarian's head
headlines.summary: The Supreme Court ruled that a Louisiana prisoner whose dreadlocks were forcibly shaved off by prison guards cannot sue the guards under a federal law to protect the religious rights of prisoners.

headlines.title: In symbolic vote, Congress directs Trump to remove forces from Iran war
headlines.summary: The measure to remove U.S. armed forces from hostilities with Iran does not require the president's signature, nor does it carry the force of law. But it reflects bipartisan frustration with the war.

headlines.title: Congress passes the largest housing affordability bill in decades
headlines.summary: An expansive bipartisan bill intended to bring down the cost of housing by boosting the supply of homes has passed both houses of Congress, and is headed to the president's desk for a signature.

headlines.title: Is AI 'one big bubble'? Behind the tech sell-off
headlines.summary: Investors are selling off AI-related stocks as doubts are starting to surface over whether the massive spending on AI is worth the investment and whether it's "one big bubble."

headlines.title: Extreme heat, storms cause chaos in Europe
headlines.summary: Two children aged two and four are among 18 people who have died so far in a heat wave across France, as large parts of Europe struggle to deal with record-breaking summer temperatures.

headlines.title: Aid agencies call for children-focused Ebola response
headlines.summary: Global humanitarian groups are urging governments and partners to place children at the center of the response to the Ebola outbreak in the Democratic Republic of Congo, warning that conflict, malnutrition and fragile healthcare systems are putting young people at heightened risk.

headlines.title: Iran, US peace talks advance with progress
headlines.summary: Progress appears to be holding in the technical talks between the United States and Iran in Switzerland, with the establishment of four working groups to oversee the implementation of the mechanisms of their interim peace agreement, according to Iranian Deputy Foreign Minister Kazem Gharibabadi.

headlines.title: Pages of friendship: Fresh insights into China from US teens
headlines.summary: What do US high school students jot down in a blank travel notebook during their China trip? The Great Wall, the Forbidden City, new Chinese friends, shared hobbies, or their new insights into the country? Join China Daily's Xia Ji to flip through their travel journal and see China from a youthful perspective!

headlines.title: Kenyans eye tariff-free boom with Chinese market
headlines.summary: Kenya is intensifying efforts to expand its presence in China's vast consumer market, with exporters seeking to significantly increase shipments of avocados, coffee, tea and other agricultural products following Beijing's introduction of zero-tariff access for goods from African countries with which it has diplomatic relations.

headlines.title: IT Ministry asks CJP’s Dipke to be present for hearing on X account ban
headlines.summary: IT Ministry summons CJP's Abhijeet Dipke for a hearing on the ban of their X account amid ongoing protests.

headlines.title: Supreme Court stays premature release of Bowbazar blast convict
headlines.summary: Supreme Court halts premature release of Bowbazar blast convict, deeming his actions as "almost terrorist activity" and a mastermind role.

headlines.title: Iran-U.S. MoU has had a positive impact on energy, fertiliser flow to India: MEA
headlines.summary: Iran-U.S. MoU boosts energy and fertilizer shipments to India, with 11 vessels transiting the Strait of Hormuz since June 17.

headlines.title: Vijay Amritraj, Mammootty, Rohit Sharma, Alka Yagnik take home Padma awards
headlines.summary: President Droupadi Murmu confers Padma Awards to Mammootty, Vijay Amritraj, and Alka Yagnik among 65 esteemed recipients.

headlines.title: Farmers’ group flags ration risk for lakhs in West Bengal
headlines.summary: Supreme Court directs petitioners to address West Bengal's ration access issues linked to SIR at Calcutta High Court.

headlines.title: US Senate joins House in voting to halt Iran war, rebuking Trump
headlines.summary: Reuters: US Senate joins House in voting to halt Iran war, rebuking Trump.

headlines.title: Asian stocks wobble after tech-led selloff, volatility risk highlighted
headlines.summary: Reuters: Asian stocks wobble after tech-led selloff, volatility risk highlighted.

headlines.title: MSCI extends Indonesia review to November, flags possible downgrade
headlines.summary: Reuters: MSCI extends Indonesia review to November, flags possible downgrade.

headlines.title: US presses Meta to agree to AI reviews as security concerns rise, NYT reports
headlines.summary: Reuters: US presses Meta to agree to AI reviews as security concerns rise, NYT reports.

headlines.title: Few in US say Iran war was worth it; Trump approval ties lowest of term, Reuters/Ipsos poll finds
headlines.summary: Reuters: Few in US say Iran war was worth it; Trump approval ties lowest of term, Reuters/Ipsos poll finds.

hackerNews.title: FUTO Swipe – A new swipe typing model
hackerNews.summary: Fast, accurate swipe typing system. Use it today in FUTO Keyboard, our fully offline Android keyboard app. Or download the models and build with it.

hackerNews.title: In memory of the man who put red and green squiggles under words
hackerNews.summary: Starting in Word and expanding to nearly every other word processor, and even things that aren't word processors.

hackerNews.title: Swift Package Index joins Apple
hackerNews.summary: swiftpackageindex.com: Swift Package Index joins Apple.

hackerNews.title: Extreme Heat conference cancelled due to extreme heat warning
hackerNews.summary: We regret that this event has been cancelled due to the red extreme heat warning issued by the UK Met Office.

hackerNews.title: Show HN: TikZ Editor – WYSIWYG editor for figures in LaTeX
hackerNews.summary: Free WYSIWYG editor for TikZ diagrams in LaTeX. Drag elements and the TikZ code updates instantly, without disturbing your formatting.

hackerNews.title: Meta Pauses Employee-Tracking Program Following Internal Data Leak
hackerNews.summary: The move comes after the company left potentially sensitive data from the initiative exposed internally.

hackerNews.title: The Coming Loop
hackerNews.summary: Loops, harnesses, and why even loop skeptics may end up with them.
